risk_rationale: The executive order poses a material compliance gap as it mandates new data protection measures to prevent foreign exploitation of US user data, which may require immediate product changes to align with national security directives.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Employee access to US data limited to US-based personnel (CFIUS commitment)
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Regulatory program for data protection to prevent foreign exploitation of data
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The executive order may require additional measures beyond current employee access restrictions to prevent foreign exploitation of US user data.

remediations.title: Enhance Data Protection Measures
remediations.description: Implement additional data protection protocols to prevent foreign exploitation of US user data, potentially including enhanced monitoring and access controls.

business_impact: Failure to comply with the executive order could result in significant legal and financial repercussions, including potential fines and operational restrictions.
recommended_actions: Conduct a detailed impact assessment of the executive order on current data protection measures.
Engage with legal and policy teams to interpret the executive order's requirements.
Develop and implement enhanced data protection protocols to align with the executive order.
